trade.summary: Malaysia exports electronics, palm oil, LNG, and rubber products to the UAE. The UAE exports refined petroleum, gold, and chemicals to Malaysia. The UAE is Malaysia's largest Arab trading partner. A CEPA was signed in 2022 to further boost bilateral trade.

diplomatic.summary: Malaysia and UAE maintain warm relations supported by Islamic solidarity and growing economic complementarity. The CEPA signed in 2022 elevated the relationship. Both countries cooperate in OIC, ASEAN-GCC forums, and on halal industry standards. Malaysia's Khazanah has invested in UAE assets.

military.summary: Defense cooperation is modest. Malaysia and UAE have engaged in defense industrial discussions. UAE has shown interest in Malaysian defense exports. No significant arms transfers or joint exercises.

financial.summary: Mubadala has invested in Malaysian infrastructure, tech, and financial services. Khazanah Nasional has invested in UAE assets. Both countries are leaders in Islamic finance, and collaboration in sukuk markets and Shariah-compliant investment products is deep.

energy.summary: Malaysia (via Petronas) is a major LNG producer and supplier. The UAE imports some Malaysian LNG. Both are significant energy exporters, leading to some competition in Asian markets. Renewable energy cooperation is growing.

scientific.summary: Collaboration in Islamic finance research, halal industry standards, and technology. Malaysian and UAE universities have exchange programs. Both countries cooperate on digital economy and fintech initiatives.
